## touch-browser v0.2.2

This patch release hardens CLI-only automation after direct local agent use of the browser action surface.

### Fixed

- Return a non-zero process exit code when an action result is `rejected`, so shell scripts and CLI-only agents cannot miss policy-blocked actions.
- Report missing stable refs as `target ref not found in current snapshot` instead of the misleading link-specific `follow target ref has no href` message.

### Changed

- Added a local fixture example to `touch-browser help open`, including `TOUCH_BROWSER_REPO_ROOT` for source-checkout development.
- Bumped Rust workspace, CLI runtime, MCP npm package, MCP server descriptor, and workflow client metadata to `0.2.2`.

### Verification

- Local `pnpm run quality:ci` passed.
- Local CLI smoke checks confirmed rejected actions exit with code `1`, missing refs produce the corrected message, and `touch-browser --version` reports `0.2.2`.
- Main GitHub Actions quality and SonarQube checks passed for `516a7d3`.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/nangman-infra/touch-browser/compare/v0.2.1...v0.2.2
